title: arnica alpina subsp. sornborgeri
intro: Arnica alpina subsp. sornborgeri, commonly known as Mountain Arnica or Alpine Arnica, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and pain-relieving properties. The medicinal properties of Arnica alpina subsp. sornborgeri have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing swelling and pain, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Arnica alpina subsp. sornborgeri is often prepared as a topical cream, ointment, or tincture, which can be applied directly to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
